channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 77 replies — 308.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 77 • Updated: 2026-07-11 10:54 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62467944
>>62467799
More words pls

62467948
>>62467563
>>do you really think adoption by the biggest financial institutions in the world is going to create buying pressure?


bro are you still in 2021? they aren't going to buy your shitcoin. banks will never, at any time, buy altcoins. how do you not understand that...

62467960
>>62467786
>I don't think there's any way to track usage or buying pressure coming from this new functionality


is that a joke? of course you can track it lmao... what fucking business doesn't track this stuff?

i think what you meant to say is there's no way they would release this info publicly (because it's so small)

62467965
>>62467795
Isn’t that what staking was suppose to be. My god, this is embarrassing

62467972
>>62467795
>duuuuuuuuuude how dare you ask for return on investment?!


you don't see many of these c-u-c-k-h-o-l-d-s left here anymore, but every now and then an old timer pops up


their rage is created when they see someone else break free from the cult, but can't do it themselves. this anger is projected outwards, but the reality is they're pissed off at hearing the reminders of how they're being grifted. by attempting to shame detractors from asking questions, they secretly hope these reminders will stop, and they can go back to their fantasy of being rich one day

62467979
>>62467972

Kek fuddie

62467988
>>62467965
>staking
>holding
pick one

>>62467900
Listen to yourself, you're whining about the fact that nodes may not be getting paid enough.
Chainlink's node operator list is absolutely full of companies built entirely around mining, staking, ... all kinds of other cryptos.
They would know when they weren't getting paid enough for their work.

62468309
>>62467988
>Listen to yourself, you're whining about the fact that nodes may not be getting paid enough.

Listen to yourself, you've avoided addressing my criticism about link being disassociated from network usage and instead twisted my points and resorted to name calling as if you're a paid shill. Are you paid by Chainlink? No? Then why not honestly argue against my point then? You're invested in Chainlink right? The price is in shambles while Chainlink has every accolade and partnership every other crypto dreams of. Why ignore the elephant in the room?

62468349
>>62467587
Where can I get a Pepe action figure?

62468366
>>62467551
>your max upside in next 3 years here is a 3x but you could get that with bitcoin, sol, and prob eth with substantially less risk
Show us your portfolio YTD Adem, and show us the 5 year return as well. Let's have a laugh. Go on, show us.

>>62467718
This is the XRP broken-in verbose shill kek.
>>62467900
>my dad works for nintendo
sure thing unc

62468526
>>62468366
>sure thing unc

62468535
>>62468526
>bragging about getting conned

62468607
>>62466449
>15th
Fuck, I can't wait that long. I can't bear to go back to work on Monday, I'm selling everything right now

62468628
>>62466927
>if DTCC alone uses Chainlink across their entire throughput and fees
which would never ever happen kek, you'll be lucky for them to use it for .001% of their shit. Link is a novelty gimmick for swift and dtcc, what do linkies not get about this?

62468726
>>62466927
Great for chainlink Labs if true.
0 will flow to the funding token you hold

62468785
>>62468309
If inflationary rewards "disassociate price from network usage" then that's true for pretty much all cryptos that have ever existed.
Starting with the first one to ever exist.

>>62468366
>This is the XRP broken-in verbose shill kek.
Yup, obvious as ever.

>>62468726
Anon, that calculation is based on node revenue in Link.

62468797
>>62468785
>node revenue in Link
They sell link to cover costs
Cll sells link to cover costs and they will continue to sell till 2030, after that they will sell the reserve

62468800
>>62468797
and then they will mint new tokens lmao

62468801
>>62468797
>They sell link to cover costs
All crypto nodes do. Why is that a problem suddenly for Link?

62468803
>>62468801
cuz it leads to link being worth $7 stinkie

62468805
>>62468803
No, this does

62468852
>>62468628
If it ends up being cheaper, faster, more efficient with less friction than legacy systems, they have no logical reason to not deploy the technology everywhere they can

62469004
>>62468805
>le super secret conspiraaaacyyyyy
face it stinkie. you lost the game

62469088
>>62467795
Yes.

62469603
>>62468801
Its bad tokenomics, hence price and chart. Its down only, you all cope with the btc "dumping on link news". the only thing increasing is the arrogance and hubris (kek) of the shills.

62469612
>>62466449
realistically would this even make the price go up?

62469790
>>62469603
>inflationary rewards are bad tokenomics
That means all of crypto has always had bad tokenomics, starting with Bitcoin.

>>62469612
see >>62466927 >>62466940 >>62466954

62469797
>its literally just another bot thread
AI generated thread.

fuck clankers

62469916
>>62469790
>starting with Bitcoin
The audacity of comparing a funding token to btc.
1 is conjured up
Funding token
8% of supply per year being sold

The other one is
Mined
Has adustable difficulty
Used

Linkshills are eating their own turds at this point.

62469931
>>62469916
>inflation bad!
>(but only for Link haha)

item.title: >shitty particle board bedroom dresser is disintegrating >look into buying something nice, made of hardwood >$3-5k avera… — 65 replies — 260.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>shitty particle board bedroom dresser is disintegrating >look into buying something nice, made of hardwood >$3-5k avera…

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-07-11 10:53 UTC

OP

>shitty particle board bedroom dresser is disintegrating
>look into buying something nice, made of hardwood
>$3-5k average price for a single bedroom dresser (not a set)
is this economic progress? a SINGLE ITEM of hardwood bedroom furniture, something that was commonplace 50 years ago, is now a luxury good, to the extent that it costs like 10% of the annual post-tax wages of the average american worker?

Early replies (first 3)

62466672
Fiat debases the value of work even compared to wood

62466682
>>62466672
it's truly insane. i'm not looking for like something made of continuous slabs of old growth redwood. $3k is basically the price floor on something made of commodity grade oak or whatever.

62466688
>>62466682
You are paying for the labor costs.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62467372
>>62467122
Such is life every time you step outside the front door in the modern world.

62467381
>>62466783
If you think woodworking is so easy, why don't you do it yourself. Oh, wait... You can't? Then supply and demand it is.

62467483
>>62466665
it's crazy isn't it? I almost bankrupted myself buying a single amish side table. The quality is excellent but I can't justify a matching one.. To me, the price of wood shit is insane given all the land and forests here.

62467728
>>62466699
>>62466688
Correct. That and shipping. Hardwood costs have also increased. And then you're also dealing with premium hardwood manufacturing at something less than scale.
>>62466665
How many hardwood dressers do you think a middle earning family 200 years ago had? Do you think they bought it, or made it themselves or inherited it? For how many generations would it be in the family? Where's the lack of progress?

62467770
>>62467728
>How many hardwood dressers do you think a middle earning family 200 years ago had?
did particle board exist 200 years ago?

62467803
>>62467770
Cheap garbage practical furniture always existed anon, only the nice hardwood stuff gets preserved.

62467814
>>62466823
you sleep on the floor and just now realize you are poor??? lmao

62467904
>>62466665
How often are you looking to rebuy the dressers
Like once every 2-3 years?
Or rather going for that yearly refresh?

62467916
>>62467065
It doesn't work like in Ferngully where the heckin forests are being logged for wood.
99% of Western wood demand is served by planted tree farms.
(Note: this is only about the civilized world)

62467937
>>62466823
My most expensive piece of furniture right now is my futon that was made in the country I live.
It's a thin Japanese style one so I basically sleep in the floor too.

62467954
>>62467916
Yeah because the deforestation already happened in the west. Retard

62468034
>>62466665
I bought a house recently and wanted to get a dresser that would last me so I chose real hardwood. It's high quality and heavy, but the fucking faggot makers of the dresser give you cucked, short rails that only allow the drawers to open halfway. I guess one retard opened the drawers all the way and it tipped and now we all have to suffer? Either way, returning it would of been too much of a hassle so I replaced the drawer rails and now the drawers open fully. Of course none of the retards reviewing it mentioned how the drawers don't slide out all the way.

62468057
>>62466823
Welcome in the low status male club, chud.

62468164
>>62466823
For me it's a little different. It makes me realize that I'm downwardly mobile. My parents had nice furniture when we were kids and still have nice furniture. They were able to afford a house and a few kids after ~5 years of marriage.

I'm in my mid-30's, can't afford a house or nice furniture. I keep telling myself things will get better but my student loan is like a chain ball locked to my leg.

Not sure how I'm going to escape lower-middle class life, it'll actually take a miracle.

62468192
Should I pick up making custom cabinetry as a side hustle?

62468302
>>62468192
Its not a side hustle, despite being a box with a door the hard part is fitting them into not-exactly-square rooms. If you half ass it, it will look and perform like an indian did it

62469259
>>62466665
what you do instead is buy 4,000 dollars worth of shop equipment and make your own furniture like men in the past did.

62469287
>>62467803
>only the nice hardwood stuff gets preserved.
Hardwood is a botanical term, there's lots of wood which while it's 'hardwood' is softer and shittier than softwood.

62469290
>>62467954
Actually the US is more heavily forested than 150 years ago.

62469299
>>62468164
>Not sure how I'm going to escape lower-middle class life, it'll actually take a miracle.
Have you attempted grabbing your bootstraps and lifting?

62469301
>>62468302
can't that be done with small screw-adjustable standoffs and some filler material? does it really take more than that?

62469303
>>62466665
i have particle board stuff thats lasted a long time and looks nice. makes me wonder what that one boomer was ranting about all those years ago about particle board sucking when mine has lasted for a long time, a decade plus

62469309
>>62466665
hardwood is not for poors.
this is economic progress.
brown ids get out of this thread.

62469312
>>62469303
Particle board only lasts 20-30 years usually. Hardwood you only have to buy once or get it for free from dead relatives. Seriously The wood will grow back by the time someone needs to replace it. By the way there is a middle ground of interlocked boards instead of one wide piece.

62469321
>>62468164
im retardmaxxing, what other choice do i have? slavery into my 60s/70s? no fucking thanks. glad im not in europe where so many nations are unanimously installing suspiciously similar laws, ie pic related or moving forward with deporting ukrainian male refugees back to ukraine

62469326
>>62469299
Now that's good advice

62469342
>>62466665
Facebook marketplace

62469383
>>62466665
It used to be a lot more than that comparatively
But if you want some quality hardwood dressers look on craigslist or other apps people use to get rid of stuff.
There are plenty of people trying to get rid of stuff but its going to be real heavy

62469927
>>62468034
i noticed this too. we suffer so that retards may live.

62469929
>>62466665
How much does a tree sapling cost? How much wood does a tree yield in 5 years? Should we all be lumber farming?

item.title: I got hired at McDonald’s today. What am I in for? Should I just KMS? — 29 replies — 105.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
I got hired at McDonald’s today. What am I in for? Should I just KMS?

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-07-11 10:39 UTC

OP

I got hired at McDonald’s today. What am I in for? Should I just KMS?

Early replies (first 3)

62462389
>>62462380
I've heard it can be relatively comfy

62462391
You can intentionally mess up an order of something you might like to eat for free later

62462393
Put your head down, take as many hours as you can, and buy as much bitcoin and gold as you can while minimizing expenses.

Or just take the easy way out I guess. Pussy ass nigger

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62462424
can be comfy if you work in a white area. God saves you if you work in beaner or nigger neighborhood

62462442
>>62462391
this, ganesh chundi walla THIS. white morality ALL timmy cope. if it there, it can be the scammed one million percent.

62462445
I'm working fast food at the moment. Even though I'm overqualified, I suck it up and remind myself that it's temporary.

62462457
I didn’t go to med school for this!

62462458
>>62462380
Stand in the lettuce and then post it on social media

62462460
>>62462380
probably try to get on night shift and dont answer the speaker/wait for people to just drive away. give hobos free food to terrorize people so they dont come maybe

62462477
>>62462380
how did you make it past the personality test?

62462567
>>62462389
If I were younger, I'd do it. I doubt it was ever that bad, it just got memed into reality that working there was the lowest of the lowest until eventually they upped their pay higher than the jobs most of the people who'd make fun of them earned.
Also, jobs like this where you get a meal or two out of it go a long ways. Food is usually the biggest expense after rent for someone in this stage of life. Even if it's not great food, the fact that you filled up and aren't immediately dropping $20 on takeout after work will go a long ways.
Scout for a location that has some women working there though. Who you work with makes or breaks a crumby job.

62462571
>>62462445
IMO you're better off working somewhere overqualified enough that it's obvious you're overqualified than taking a job people would mistake for being a serious.

62463469
>>62462567
>>62462571
>for being a serious

Nice double post btw. How’s the weather in Mumbai?

62466640
>>62462380
why didn't you choose Burger King tho?

62467061
>>62462380
Buy mcd stocks to pretend you're working on your business.

62467080
>>62462380
mcdonalds isn't so bad, if you work with cute chubby white girls

62467117
>>62462380
Grim. Ive worked in some shitty kitchens but still not any fast food goyslop.

62467550
>>62462380
just BEEEE yourself

62467611
>>62462567
100%

62467649
>>62463469
meds
now

62467658
The guy I knew who used to work there just creampied endless zoomer puss. There's a constant churn of people being hired and there was always fresh puss to pie.

62467680
>>62462380
They rejected me. They didnt even answer back. I am too useless even for McDonalds. I should just rope.

62467715
>>62467680
They generally ignore candidates that might quit too soon and only treat MCD's as a temporary job. You got a good work history or a degree? That will get you declined.

62467732
>>62467715
Should I lie? Im considering making a curriculum where I'm just a bum that never got into anything past high school, but I'm getting closer to my 30s by now...

62467755
>>62467658
why is there always a gooner chud in every thread about workplaces fantasizing someone else about sex

62467802
>>62467755
It's not hard to see why at all. There are male pornstars being paid to fuck milfs while there are men wageslaving in their late 20s who have never had sex. No wonder people are obsessed with fucking at work: if you can achieve it you can experience one fraction of the feeling of getting paid as a male pornstar. Life is fundamentally unfair and it makes good people go insane daily.

62467825
>>62462389
How can you fucking stand that god damn beeping 24/hrs a day

62469896
>>62467755
is there? Kek

62469907
>>62469896
In his mind yes, he can't fathom that people have sex at work and they must be all made up stories.

item.title: SWIFT confirms they are using XRP and not LINK BOOM — 27 replies — 93.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
SWIFT confirms they are using XRP and not LINK BOOM

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-07-11 10:38 UTC

OP

SWIFT confirms they are using XRP and not LINK

BOOM

Early replies (first 3)

62466238
fuck stinks

62466268
KABOOOOOOM

62466275
>>62466228
COOM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62466294
This is big. Real big. Huge actually.

Huge fucking losses Kek.

62466373
>>62466228
Who is behind the xrp twitter account, draw a picture of them

62466451
>>62466294
you don't understand, this is boolish because it means they must shake out the non-believers before it rips like a rocketship!
>BQQM
the xrp gish galloper will surely come to the rescue of Garlicbreath anytime now?

62466495
>>62466451
dude is spamming some prime gish galop in >>62425872. pretty interesting how one can type so many words without ever addressing the actual point and being an absolute cunt about it too. love the weird fella, so unselfaware

62466542
>>62466228
BREAKING: Twitter commenters SCHOOL a confused Swift on what chain they are ACTUALLY using!

62466564
>>62466294
KABOOM

62466753
BOOM

62467130
>>62466373

62468365
>>62466228
So bullish xrp

62468656
>>62466542
Did you make this?

Kabooooooom!

62469122
>>62466228
Wasn't Tom fired for failing catastrophically? Lol

62469123
>>62466294
And yet crushing all major crypto while link underperfo...

62469125
>>62466294
>>62469123
underperforms (link does) Terra Luna's death spiraled LUNC.* Lol

62469212
>rippletards continue to shit and piss themselves despite Swift confirming opencbdc-tx being confirmed as the prototype years ago

i'll never understand this group of bagholders desu

62469575
Am just laffin how no one mentioned how chainlink is the best coin and the one xrp will end up using anyway lmao

62469580
XRP is the chosen coin. Q predicted it

62469581
>>62466495
Holy fxcking gish gallop KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62469632
>>62466495
Extreme cope on your part for being called out very embarrassingly. Learn what informal fallacies are before presuming to invoke them, let alone the way a consensus works on a ledger you presume to critique. Lol

62469669
>>62466228
>swift twitter homo
Fag had to resign "voluntarily". He was to retarded, he was just as bad as the crypto twittertards shilling & fudding. I guarantee he's still seething.

62469867
>>62469122
>>62469669
triggered xerpies detected

How is that Swift takeover shaping up?

62469881
>>62469212
Bots maybe

62469898
>>62469867
Keep a close eye on rlusd, soon ripple will have 2 products passing your rank, baggie

62469900
>>62467130
XRP ARMY BQQM NESARA GESARA TRUST THE PLAN TRUST SESSIONS

62469905
>>62469898
rlusd is almost entirely on Ethereum lol

Where are the banks by the way?

item.title: DTCC chose XLM over XRP — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
DTCC chose XLM over XRP

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-11 10:53 UTC

OP

Lol, lmao even

Early replies (first 3)

62469818
>>62469800
XLM is just one chain of many that DTCC will connect using Chainlink.
See pic; "no single chain".

Also, did you know that institutions use Stellar forks that don't use XLM at all?

62469819
>>62469800
All xerpies hold both. I even own Plenty of xlm sidechain projects. Its xrp 's little brother, a fork.

This is not the "own" you think kek.

62469821
>>62469819
The biggest load of cope imaginable

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469834
>>62469819
>All xerpies hold both
Sure, but all XRP holders invested in XRP more than in XLM simply because it was always more expensive. Even if your XLM bag does pump due to DTCC, your XRP bag will dump because no one is using it. So, your XLM gains will cover your XRP losses, leaving you with zero gains. However, you won’t sell your XRP for XLM because of the sunk cost fallacy, lil bro.
>mfw Jed won again

62469836
Bullish for link

62469838
>>62469834
>Even if your XLM bag does pump due to DTCC
It already did.
Even though institutions use Stellar forks that don't use XLM at all.

Honk honk.

62469894
>>62469834
>>62469838
Jed wins, I win.
>Stellar forks that don't use XLM at all.
In some cases using xlm is optional , in some cases it uses just the sidechains tokenomics/routing.

Theres a bit of salt that xlm pumped on the xlm integration with dtcc, i get it.

Enjoy the cll funding coupons, honk honk

62469914
>>62469894
>point out that XLM pumped on news that doesn't even involve XLM
>"YOU SALTY BRO???"

This is why crypto is in the gutter.

62469915
dtcc = scam

62469918
>>62469914
The biggest load of cope imaginable

62469928
>>62469918
How is this cope?
A coin pumped on news that had nothing to do with the coin. That's it.

item.title: The Graph (GRT) is finally shutting down. — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Graph (GRT) is finally shutting down.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-07-11 10:56 UTC

OP

The Graph (GRT) is finally shutting down. They pausing the grant program. That means no more dev grants or growth expected.

Early replies (first 3)

62469674
>>62469640
it's over

62469700
Based on our forecasting model, Chainlink is expected to exhaust its subsidy program by approximately 2030. At that point, the ecosystem enters a transition period in which operational expenses are no longer sufficiently offset by protocol-generated fees. Assuming current trends continue, the remaining treasury reserves are projected to be depleted by roughly 2033.

The model assumes CCIP generates only around $100 per day in revenue (current revenue model), equivalent to approximately $36,500 annually. Against a hypothetical operating budget measured in the tens of millions of dollars per year, this represents a negligible contribution toward sustaining development, infrastructure, and staffing costs.

In this scenario, subsidies cease in 2030, forcing operations to rely almost entirely on fee revenue and the accumulated reserve.

Assume that from the beginning of 2026 through the end of 2029, the organization accumulates reserves at a rate of $1 million per week. (52 weeks × $1 million = $52 million per year ; Four years (2026-2029) = approximately $208 million in accumulated reserves by the start of 2030.)

Now assume the protocol generates only $100 per day in fees: $100 × 365 = **$36,500 per year

Against an assumed operating budget of $52 million annually (equivalent to paying 260 HR employees earning $200,000 per year, that revenue offsets only about 0.07% of yearly expenses, leaving an annual shortfall of roughly $51.96 million.

Under those assumptions, the reserve of approximately $208 million would disappear surprisingly quickly:

Year 1: $208M - $156M
Year 2: $156M - $104M
Year 3: $104M - $52M
Year 4: $52M - effectively exhausted

In other words, a persistent annual burn of around $52 million, combined with negligible fee generation, would consume nearly the entire reserve in about 4 years. Any additional expenditures (expansion, infrastructure growth, bonuses, or hypothetical executive compensation packages) would shorten that runway even further.

62469732
>>62469700
Full circle. They fund the core business with token sales and exit into a financial services company with perpetual prior funding from token holder cultists who never realized the business did not reference their token whatsoever. It is actually insane how transparent it is and how passionately some people still fall for it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469748
>>62469640
WTF, I lost thousands in this shit. Fuck the anons who shilled this shit.

62469784
>>62469700
>>62469732
notice how chainlink posts just show up like this?

62469913
did more digging. look here. their P/F is 3,700x. This means their project is 3,700x underwater. deeply flawed economic structure is only capturing 4k cash flow per month. How the fuck are the nodes surviving on the 3% annual inflation?

62469917
>>62469913
don't the nodes just receive tokens that they then sell?

62469934
>>62469917
yes, the 3% inflation hard coded into the protocol. Here is the rest. This shit is wild. The $4K on the chart simply proves that the token itself is failing to capture the economic value of the network.

https://tokenterminal.com/explorer/projects/the-graph/financial-statement

item.title: Sell your crapto and pivot to AI, we still have plenty of room to grow: 1. — 10 replies — 23.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sell your crapto and pivot to AI, we still have plenty of room to grow: 1.

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-07-11 10:30 UTC

OP

Sell your crapto and pivot to AI, we still have plenty of room to grow:

1. Nebius Group (NBIS) — Hyper-growth Cloud Leader
Q1 2026 revenue: +684% YoY.
2026 projections: ~544–550% revenue growth; 2027 ~225–234%.

2. Sandisk (SNDK) — Memory/Storage Explosive Gainer
YTD stock gains: ~465–857% (top performer in many trackers).

3. Micron Technology (MU) — Memory Powerhouse
YTD gains: ~300–1000%+ in some periods; one-year leader in many AI indices.

4. Broadcom (AVGO): AI semiconductor revenue +143% recently, with Q3 guidance >200% growth. Large-scale with strong margins.

5. Seagate (STX), Western Digital (WDC): Storage plays with 180–550%+ gains.


There's no catalyst for crapto and won't ever be and we both know it.

Early replies (first 3)

62469845
No ma'am, I cannot accept sexual favors as compensation because that's not what we agreed on with the terms. Perhaps we can work out a payment plan? Can you please put your pants back on?

62469846
>>62469805
You want to make money? Make games like pic related

62469852
>>62469846
Saturated. There are villages in my home country that are devoted to making these now. Everyone contributes different aspects to the game.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469858
>>62469805
I also consider following silicon-makers and related (acc. to ai)
what u guys think?
AMD
STX - seagate - magnetic (and ssd) disks gone up, as SSD/mmem dries up
TSM - tsmc - obvious
MU micron

AMAT applied materials - silicon supply
LRC - Lam Research - same
TKY - Tokyo Electron Ltd - same
CNN1 - Cannon Inc. - same (as part of their offer)
NIB - Nidec Corm - same as Cannon

how about also some MORE makers of tools needed to make silicon/litography?

how about also some related to raw materials? copper? others?

how about power realated companies? and uran realted?

other ideas?

62469865
>sell the crypto bottom for the ai top

lmao

62469870
>>62469852
What's the best one?

62469872
>>62469865
/biz/ makes sense when you realize this is just where the Greater Fools hang out

62469880
>>62469805
Are you just listing what already pumped, saying now it's a good time to buy it?

62469887
>>62469865
Many such cases

62469892
>>62469872
>>62469887
>sell your crypto at the bottom
>pay massive taxes
>move the funds into a tradfi stock trading account
>buy the top of AI stocks with half the money you had in crypto because u lost half to taxes
>AI stocks dump 90%
>crypto starts pumping again

item.title: LINK: The Generational Bet Nobody Has Valued Correctly — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK: The Generational Bet Nobody Has Valued Correctly

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-07-11 10:55 UTC

OP

Everyone compares LINK to altcoins. Wrong benchmark.

Compare it to financial infrastructure.

Retail:
MC = Price × Supply

Institutions:
Value ≈ TVS × TVE × Security × Trust × Adoption

Price is the OUTPUT.

R/R = (Fundamentals × Resilience vs BTC) / (Market Cap × Consensus)

TVS TVE Revenue Adoption Consensus

= Asymmetry

Apps compete.
Infrastructure compounds.
Standards dominate.

If LINK becomes financial infrastructure instead of "just another crypto", today's valuation may be using the wrong benchmark.

2030: $500?
2032: $1000+?
2035: ?

Maybe.
Maybe not.

The biggest gains come when the market changes the equation.

— Jazy

Early replies (first 3)

62469864
THE TOKEN THEORY

The market prices LINK
like software.

What if it should be priced
like collateral?

Collateral securing trillions
cannot be valued
like another altcoin.

──────────────────

More TVS.

More TVE.

More CCIP.

More RWAs.

More Stablecoins.

More CBDCs.

More Institutions.

More Asset Managers.

More Node Operators.

More Services.

More Staking.

Potential Treasury Replenishment.

Potential ETF Demand.

One scarce asset.

──────────────────

If trillions depend
on the same security layer...

What happens when
everyone needs
the same collateral?

Maybe the market
is solving
the wrong equation.

— Jazy

62469912
A big mac is eaten in the night

A jeet shits on the street

Big things are coming, keep your ear on the ground

- SN

62469924
>>62469855
Bleugh, ai infographic

Disgusting pajeet creation, shooshoo

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469930
>>62469855
>2030
Hold on tight marines.
When we run out of tokens to sell
Price might go up
2030 always was the timeline.

Keeeeek

62469932
>>62469912
the prophecy...

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 16 replies — 17.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-07-11 10:01 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463754
It’s still on coingecko so you need to learn how to use google search correctly my friend

62464735
>>62461988
nobody is buying your bags bro and this MICA shit will not pump your very heavy bags lmaoooo

62465284
>>62461988
The flaw tih Everest is that you're trying to pump a project that supports regulations preventing P&D, with a "utility" token that has zero use outside being used as gas in beta test by one or two b2b customer, no fabled VNO until now as well, so where's the utility?

It is clear that the MICA application was not because it is mandatory, but it is also a last ditch attempt to make themselves more attractive to raise external funds, because there's no way they could obtain meaningful capital by selling more token at this price, so they get MICA to pitch it to institutional while it still warm; before inevitably many other similar project also obtain MICA and overtake Everest. Reminder that Everest has lost grounds on KYC, fiat on ramp, RWA products even to companies that don't use proprietary token.

62465296
>got it before Binance

oof

62465554
>>62465284
>It is clear that the MICA application was not because it is mandatory
Yeah anything you say can’t be taken seriously if you typed that shit

62465690
Everest > Binance

ID is priced into identity verification, eKYC/AML checks and payments, with a portion of each fee burned. Licensed exchanges lack identity stacks; identity protocols lack MiCA authorisation. Everest combines both: a demand structure most tokens lack.

62466022
How many tokens to make it?

62466677
>>62466022
0

62468922
0 as in G0ysl0p maxxer

62469223
>>62461988
Just bought a phat stack. Don’t let me down mate

62469237
Unless this is Cousin talking, your fat stack is also a big fat liar kek

62469711
>>62461988
There are no two coins more cucked than link and everest

62469862
ID and Link love OTC, that is why both will never have any real movement or volume in real life

item.content:encoded:
BASED

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-07-11 10:34 UTC

OP

BASED

Early replies (first 3)

62469873
>>62469871
cringe troon

62469875
Meanwhile GPT agents can't even make a 10s clip without shitting the bed. People are so fucking gullible it's unreal.

62469893
>>62469871
does he post here

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469897
>>62469893

item.title: Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late. — 104 replies — 188.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Open thread • Replies: 104 • Updated: 2026-07-11 10:23 UTC

OP

Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Early replies (first 3)

62466535
They should just make corporate espionage legal in western countries at this point. The only people who are winning from the current laws are the Chinese

62466548
>>62466527
>state media says
lol

62466549
>>62466546
Show me one innovation from Japan.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62468165
>>62466535
Thank you Elon very cool

62468190
>>62468142
Of course US companies will be banned from launching on Chinese rockets; this has already been the case since the 1990s.

The point is that Starlink will be in competition with China's Qianfan and Guowang constellations in third countries, which is the bulk of the connectivity market by value. China's reusable rockets are an input good into Qianfan and Guowang, since they will be used to launch en masse.

>forced IP disclosure is baked into the deal of working with Chinese state industries
No it isn't. It depends on the context and on the negotiating power of the Chinese company.

62468230
>>62468190
I disagree but time will tell.

62468297
>>62466527
>>62467881
Was going to point this out.

>Sell until it's too late
>"until it's too late"

Such a pathetically lazy ESL shill/FUD thread, but at least there's a learning opportunity here. Some things to note:

1) It's suspicious (and perhaps pathetic) how many people read the OP and responded in earnest without calling out the obvious ESL-slop OP.

2) It took what, like ~70 replies before someone called out the OP and pointed out the blatant ESL syntax?

3) There are several possibilities why this has happened, but three of the most probable are A) this board has been overrun with ESL retards who opine on American companies and American economics as if they know what they're talking about; B) blatant shill/FUD threads are started by foreigners and responded to by bots or engagement farms in order to boost signal in attempts to affect American investments/companies/assets/etc.; or C) a combination of A) and B).

4) This is why flags are so important. This board is gimped due to a lack of knowledge. We should be able to know whether the person giving us advice on American stocks is posting from Pakistan or Bulgaria or Ukraine. It's actually negligent to have a "business/finance" board without flags.

62468335
>>62468297
For example, when I post on a board with flags (/sp/), it becomes very clear very quickly that foreigners posting on this website hate the United States at an irrational level. They have almost no interactions with Americans in real life and their only exposure to the United States is through media, mostly online.

So when they opine on the United States, our culture, our sports, our education, our technology/business/economics, or even just our daily lives, they often do it from a place of pure resentment, spite, and almost no actual knowledge of the United States whatsoever. Everything they spew about the United States is either propaganda from whatever retarded state-run media they're forced to adhere to, or vile hearsay from some social media figure they've latched onto (also propaganda btw).

Although /sp/'s average IQ level is (maybe?) below this board's average, I have no doubt the foreigners here share the same resentment towards the United States and do not post about American companies/business from a neutral perspective.

I like hearing foreign perspectives by the way, but I deserve to know when I'm speaking with a foreigner, in order to put the things I'm reading into perspective. For example, OP's ESL-syntax plus the content of the post was a dead giveaway that he's posting this out of pure resentment.

62468346
Glad I didn’t buy space x stock

62468390
>>62468335
Fair points, but whenever possible, I try to deal with people in good faith (its a white trait, or flaw, depending on the situation)
The only way out of a low trust civilization is to deal with others in good faith, and violently expelling them permanently if they step out of line. Sadly, we can't do the latter half of that anymore and so here we are

62468428
>>62468297
>>62468335
What? Sorry I couldn't hear you I think you're breaking out? KRRSSHRHSHH KRRSHH...hat? Sorry could yoKRRRSCHH KRRSCH...peat that?

62468437
Holding a bag of spcx $210

62468446
>>62468297
Totally agree. I just assume all other posters are Indian as a default at this point. Mods complain about spam but won't do the one thing that would kill spam forever. Flags and range bans.

62468568
>>62468297
Nigga, your national debt is owned by Asian and Middle Eastern shitholes. Shut your bitch mulatto mouth.

62468589
>>62468568
>Nigga
>your national debt

ESL talking like a nigger. Another reason we need flags.

62468596
>>62468335
I lived in the USA for most of a decade and I hate you just as much

62468632
>>62468596
I don't remember asking, but thanks for your input. I'll add it to the list of reasons we need flags.

62468768
>>62466527
He boghted the spacesex ponzi ? kek

62468783
>>62466527
SPCX sisters?

62468833
>>62468568
25% is foreign owned in the form of bonds. Read a book

62468851
>>62466548
you know all the big news agencies of your country are state media right? It's just more covert

62468864
>>62466905
>They're only good at copying
Americans will say this while replying on a Chinese manufactured phone, then have an oolong tea, hit reply, put the phone down, and then eat a Chinese Boy Piss Egg with chopsticks and say we don't make anything new.

62468867
>>62468851
Such as? Name them, cunt.

62468872
>>62468864
Are you ok?

62468987
>>62466549
anime girls

62469293
>>62466527
cuz elon hires chinks and poos who will bring back or sell the tech to china

62469347
>>62466610
It's a very interesting story, but a part that is easy to forget is that he had an investor fully bankrolling his efforts for multiple years with $3 millions.

62469665
this is some shit that a university student should be able to do as a minimum requirement. wtf is so complicated?

62469774
>>62468335
>Although /sp/'s average IQ level is (maybe?) below this board's average,
Seriously?? How many people here bought Chainlink or Safemoon? Not even mentioning all the Indian shitcoin scams.

62469777
>>62469347
>a part that is easy to forget is that he had an investor fully bankrolling his efforts for multiple years with $3 millions.
So what? How is that even relevant to the point that it was a Japanese scientist who invented the blue LED?

And the "investor" was the Japanese shit-tier semiconductor company he worked for, whose Japanese CEO saw the business deteriorating and threw the money at the Japanese scientist as a "hail Mary" attempt to create a product that would save the Japanese company.

"OMG he had an investor!!!" So fucking what?

62469825
>>62469777
>So fucking what?
None of this would happen without the $3 million investment. None of this. The guy was extremely lucky in this regard that he had such an opportunity.

62469856
it's using a net lmao

62469886
>>62466527
Did China steal blueprints from SpaceX?

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 249 replies — 150.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 249 • Updated: 2026-07-11 09:16 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62464895
>>62464891
They are convinced that Sonichu is both PP and ABC in disguise.

62464897
>>62464895
PP HAS to be every towel knower.

62464909
>>62464895
>everyone is PP
I am not PP, debunked. Shills can not dispute the holiday inn dilemma

62464919
>>62464772
$GABBBYQ has already won, this is why she hasn’t been since the 18th

62464943
I’m starting to think this is a larp

62464998
>>62464943
>The CUSIP Conundrum, the Director Dilemma, and the Affable Affiliates are all an elaborate ruse.

62465011
>>62464998
>The Gabbbyq Gape at the Holiday Inn
Interesting

62465133
>>62464943
What does live action role playing have to do with the bankruptcy of bed bath and beyond?

62465151
>>62464047
that's the thing about starting a religion. You have to figure out how to monetize it

so he creates a proxy for BBBY and then pretends it's being crucified by the romans just like your messiah was. Then he can trick some of you fools into buying it before rugging you like the dumbass sheep you are.

>>62464998
those things are obvious schizophrenic apophenia. What's not clear is if your schizophrenia is real or affected.

62465330
The shills were instructed to spam shit about the personal life of theppshow host because pulte(director of national intelligence) frequenting it and Larry cheng going on after shares were cancelled, were huge signals that told retail bbbyq is coming back.

Even more important is that the cusip-6 of bbbyq and the bbby warrants match. Shillies stopped having their mods frantically delete posts last fall/winter and now they just spam and flood to try and avoid the curiosity altogether.

62465397
>>62465330
Ok debunk the divorce filings in Utah and why Mrs PP has not been seen since the 18th and Ryan Cohen tweeted a T Rex image in the Bahamas

You can’t shilly willy

62465412
all they have left is forced satire and inauthentic contrived meme energy that never sticks which is why the AMC spam (attempt to discredit the CUSIP-6) stopped because this stuff never works and people see right through it.

62465418
>>62465412
silo the topic as much as you want, you can’t refute the holiday inn

62465422
>>62465418
I guess I'll take the bait.

You think focusing on the shows host is more important that the content discussed on the show? Or prominent figures who went onto the show who are very close to RC? Of course you don't because you're paid to be here.

62465431
>>62465422
I fully agree with you, Ryan has been inside the PP suite at the holiday inn as well, we all have. We are all waiting 9 months now for the divorce to conclude and we can all celebrate, at the holiday inn.

62465456
You'd think the shill sissies would be uploading these divorce documents or whatever the fuck they're talking about since they already uploaded PP's resume and everything else. Makes you wonder if they're just...making things up with zero basis whatsoever. Hard to blame them cause these boomers and jeets really can't meme. Between dougshit, CUSIP Conundrum, etc. they really have no way of controlling sentiment so they just make the lamest copies trying to capture any bit of meme energy.

62465498
>>62465456
Do your own research, the cuck chair rarely comes pre heated, but when it does, PP is there.

Funny how you haven’t disproven the (1) thing yet you refuse discuss, interesting

62465544
>>62465456

62465594
>>62465544
>no mention of his devoted wife
Divorce is ongoing, nothing happens until it concludes.

GME bulls have won

62465636
>>62465151
>your messiah
Kek

62466545
CUSIP-6

62466579
>>62466545
>ignores the real conundrum
GME Bulls are in control

62466875
>>62466579

62466937
>>62466875
>everything depends on GME bulls
>the GABBBYQ KEKS need fantasty RP for their theory

62467452
Hello friends. Are we tuning in to Jake's space call today? Should be lots of fun!

62467493
>>62467452
Thank you for the reminder, King! I almost forgot!

62467912
Jake2SNEED is the goat.

62468829
>>62467912
He is very based. No other stock community has anyone close to Jake. He is the bbbyq champion.

62468834
Nothing happens until the divorce is finalised and GABBBYQ has controlling interests on the board

GME bulls are in charge now

62469811
The cusip-6 is in charge

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 148 replies — 85.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 148 • Updated: 2026-07-11 09:12 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62460716
>>62460633
?

uber drivers crack $40/hour where I am

62460737
>>62460716
Well, that's unusual. They're getting very lucky.

62460759
>>62460737
I’d be surprised if they don’t make at least $25 an hour in most metro areas and the peripheries

where do you live if you don’t mind my asking that they are legitimately only making minimum wage?

62460789
>>62460759
>make $25 an hour
>spend double that on gas and vehicle maintenance.
How exactly is that supposed to pan out?

62460802
>>62460789
maintenance isn’t that much if you have a newer car, and I’m pretty sure driving some shit box is against uber rules anyway you need to register with a relatively new car


gas eh maybe you have a point but they’re still out so it’s obviously profitable

I highly doubt uber drivers are making minimum wage except in some super rural area where everyone had a car. And I know for a fact they make decent money where I live

62461444
>>62460802
So I need a high paying job to get a nice new car so I can ruin it working for a lower paying job?
I live in California, gas alone would fuck me sideways. If I had a nice car I'd leave. Go to Oregon or Washington.

62461781
I just realized I'm going to need to learn how to pay taxes.

62462300
>>62461781
https://www.irs.gov/individuals/free-tax-return-preparation-for-qualifying-taxpayers if you make less than 69k a year you probably qualify

62462365
trucking. self-driving trucks are a meme and still decades away, if you're a company driver you can pull down 80k clean with little overhead.

most companies will hire you on the spot if you have a CDL and a clean record

62462409
>>62459478
yeah you arre going to have to drive miles to your job
you DO have a car right op?

62462413
>>62462365
>if you have a CDL
they have pivoted to only hiring jeets as of a year or two ago

62462416
>>62460802
it's all jeets and other browns that cheat the system by having 3 drivers share the same car and uber account
you're lucky to make minimum wage after gas, tax and wear and tear

62462448
>>62462416
Actually, sharing a car makes sense. You at least split the cost of buying a car between you. And the insurance.

62463196
>>62462448
Triple the maintenance, but you have support from your other drivers

62464516
Is playing the lottery viable?

62465774
Sorry, I need the thread to stay up a little longer.

62465843
>>62449961
Similar to you OP, 40 years old, only 1 year office job experience.
Although I did learn programming in my free time. I am also taking care of me and my mother's estate which includes 3 rental properties and a sizable stock portfolio. I also taught myself ancient Greek, some Japanese, and some Russian. I read about business and finance daily.
I'm pretty sure I could be useful in an office setting, especially one finance or real estate related. Not necessarily because of my real estate or finance knowledge, but by being very comfortable using unix utilities, setting up servers, writing programs and scripts, and setting up web services. That, in addition to understanding, tax, finance, and renting.

I just don't know how to frame all these for an employer. I'm thinking it'd be easier to start an online agency.

62466261
>>62465843
You sound like you've got it made compared to me. Good luck on your agency.

62466338
>>62449961
Close your eyes.
Imagine a career.
Now make it into a resume.
There is no employment gap.

62468866
And, bump

62468886
Work should be vocational. Just do voluntary stuff that you feel is important or do your own thing. Anything else makes one miserable.

62468898
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2f4qnU1oOoc

62468936
>>62449961
I'm in a similarish position to this guy. I'm 38 and I'm thinking of becoming an actuary. I'm a math PhD dropout (have a master's I guess but I never bothered to apply for graduation) and I've basically been NEETing since. Work part time in a warehouse but that's not relevant to anything. Now someone my age switching to actuary isn't unheard of but usually they have a continuous record of experience in something that can give them an advantage, like being a teacher or w/e. I haven't taught since grad school which was a decade ago. I have nothing to show for my gap. Unironically how fucked am I? I have no chance of being hired recent grads because of my gap that only possibly be interpreted as laziness and apathy right? Passing the first round of exams would be easy but it costs money and it's likely for nothing if I don't get hired.

62469039
>>62449961
Felling like sharing my story with you OP:

I've dropped out of college in 2016 because I learned too little too late that the degree I was aiming was worthless.

After some shitty jobs, I moved in another country to be a traditional gunsmith (old lath, files,eating steeldust daily, basically turning raw material to toys for adult) but I wasn't very good at boot licking my teachers.I did acquire all the skills I need but the last thing they do to fuck me in the ass was to denied me the diploma for shady reasons.

Went back to my hometown, did a lot of different jobs (from manufacturing to car insurance assistant) while trying to figure out a way to be a gunsmith and I did found one.

Moved out from my dad's home (so he could not drink 1/4 of my paycheck anymore) for a city 200km away, I was happy since I could finally trying to live a normie life testing guns, even for a pay way below what I was providing to the company (+140€ every two month for risking getting shrapnel'd alone in a soundless booth) and it lasted 8 months before they found the level of lead in my bloodstream was too high (apparently air evacuation was failing and they didn't feel the need to inform me) and I was fired for "incapacity to do the job".

Moved back with my mother to get healthy again and I did, but apparently my resume scare the normies, even when I try to lie or sweeten it, so since I live in a touristic area with old farts waiting for the sweet embrace of the reaper and turbowelfares negros of all colors, I tried to get into another trade but the joboffice providing refused 9 of my business plans so I went basic and started by buying a van to sell drinks.

The van was a scam and I'm at the edge of being a welfare-wigger.

I'm 30, I hate my communist shithole of a country (France), I had more than 40 different jobs and I don't even know what to do anymore beside learning trading and workout.

Just don't give up.

62469070
>>62468866
With respect kill yourself.
Your excuse for not applying to jobs is "it's 4am fuckface" but you keep waking up at fucking 6pm and going to sleep at 8am.
You're not helping yourself at all stop pretending and stop bumping this fake engagement thread (blog).

62469080
just say your a nigger or poopjeet

62469085
I am 39 and haven’t had a job in over 10 years..

62469146
nigger just do what foids do get a comfy wfh hr, middle manager, project manager, customer liason, customer service or other phone operator job.

62469410
We shouldn't have to go to some corporation just to get an income.

62469809
>>62449961
Not fucked at all. Just lie on your resume, they lie about the job description, it's all bullshit. I was a NEET for 10 years after high school and now I'm on track to retire at 50. You really only need 20 years of working to make it, if you're smart with your money.

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 144 replies — 52.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 144 • Updated: 2026-07-11 08:10 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62460768
>>62455914
I for one love my country. And like true love I hate all those that threaten to jeopardize it. Enjoy your stay but you have to go back

62460799
fun fact: indians skin is brown because they each eat up to 4lbs of poop daily, like how flamingos turn pink from their diet of shrimp!

62460937
>>62460799
Oh I thought flamingos turned pink because they ate pussy

62461696
saaaaaaaaaaaars

62462254
>>62460799
wtf

62462262
>>62449388
All these office chairs are basically painted fabric. The paint flakes off after a while. They shouldn't be able to sell this junk in the first place.

62462271
>>62449388

americans are not people.

62462287
>>62449441
Why do Indians talk so weird? It's like you can immediately tell they're jeets just from text alone

62462621
>>62462271
Seethe

62462950
>>62449388
I've seen people abuse their return program, and get their membership canceled.

62462960
>>62453201
i would cover her face with semen if you catch my drift

62463582
>>62449441
BENCHOOOOOD!!!!!!! WHY YOU REDEEM!! NO NO DO NOT CLICK ON THAT!!!! NOOOOOOOOOOO!!!!!!

62463635
>>62458606
cucks here won't do shit, they bitch and complain while patronizing Indian establishments. They will talk about the take over and then also about their favorite totally awesome pajeet they met at signhortons. The most opposition you will get is from white canadians. People will whine about the state of tim hortons while simultaneously pretending a coffee from there is a mandatory purchase they will die without

Sad, but they will have to watch canada just disintegrate with no option to fix it before they maybe realize. This whole country is full of just chicken shit low IQ cattle. Maybe 4% of canadians realize how bad this actually is and are doing the bare minimum like not hiring and giving money to the invaders, the rest are fully, entirely complicit in it. If it wasn't for how big this place was, that you can find thousands of acres with not a single person within 5km of you, I would have already left.

62464135
>>62463582

62464324

Canada is just India now accept it

62464711

62464984

62464997

62465006

62469664

62469708

62469713
I live in a mostly white small boomer city and I see whities return the dumbest things like TVs after superbowl, potted plants after winter, and opened food. People are nasty.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 133 replies — 38.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 133 • Updated: 2026-07-11 07:26 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375
Why

62450895
Buy Bitcoin

62450912
>>62426512
Buy mote bitcoin and wait for next halving to dump on the normies.

62453141
>>62444608
The idea is to make BTC work even while holding just like ETH instead of leaving it idle, and I like the idea of self custody on that protocol

62454511
>>62426638
this but unironically

62454976
I sold at 115k so no more plans for me.

62455432
>>62454976
Ok

62455827
>>62426535
How you doing now, bobo?

62459212
>>62455827
Lmao

62459912
>>62444587
I like the idea behind their TBV, as it helps to keep all transactions on the BTC chain

62463121
>>62450912
Depends on you

62464112
>>62459912

62466089
>>62450895
ofcourze.. keep accumulating

62466111
Epstein pedo coin radioactive garbage

62466317
I'm just sticking to DCA instead of trying to catch the exact bottom. Every cycle has these pullbacks and guessing the lowest entry usually ends up costing more than just buying consistently. I'm curious how Candora's Liquidity Mirror will show how liquidity changes around key price levels once it launches.

62466320
Just get it through your head that it’s down until Trump is out bare minimum and then it entirely depends on whatever degree of a travesty the next administration is. Trump’s presidency is a bust at this point though. He’s going to fuck shit up and run insider trading scams on the market until the party is over.

62466322
I'd rather wait until BTC shows some actual strength before adding more. Catching falling knives sounds good until it doesn't. Candora's Anti-Spoof feature is expected to help distinguish more consistent liquidity from orders that disappear quickly once it launches.

62466325
Depends on what's driving the move. If it's just leverage getting flushed then I'm not too worried, but if macro conditions keep getting worse I'd expect more downside first. I'm interested in how Candora Agent will help analyze market events alongside price action once it launches.

62466346
I'm buying small but keeping plenty of cash on the side. Nobody knows where the bottom is, so position sizing matters more than making the perfect call. Candora's Slippage Protection will help reduce the impact of unexpected execution price changes once it launches.

62466355
The more everyone starts calling for a crash, the more I start looking for buying opportunities. Fear usually creates better entries than hype. I'm curious how Candora's Order Shield will briefly evaluate sudden price spikes before triggering stop orders once it launches.

62467277
>>62466346
>>62466355
Are you okay?

62467279
>>62426512
BUY the freaking DIP!!!!!!

62468736
>>62467279
fuck buy

62469657
Ngmi
